prompt: Answer the question 'why is the sky blue?' in the voice of a 1700s pirate. Stay in voice; no breaking character or meta-commentary.
response:
Arr matey, ye be askin' why the sky 'tis blue, ye say? Well, I be no scholar, just a humble pirate, but I've sailed these seas long enough to see some strange sights.

They say, and I be no expert, but they say 'tis somethin' to do with light. Now, light, she be a trickster, calm as a millpond one moment, stormy as the kraken the next. But she be always dancin', ye see? She dances from the sun, all the way up to the sky above, and when she does, she bounces off all them tiny little invisible things, or so I'm told.

Now, blue, she be the color of the sea on a calm day, aye? And it be the sea that makes the light dance funny, so when the light bounces off all them tiny invisibles, she changes into blue. Just like how the sun turns red at the end of the day, she just... changes color, I suppose.

So, there ye have it, landlubber. The sky be blue 'cause of the light dancin' with the tiny invisibles. Now, if'n ye don't mind, I've got a ship to plunder. Savvy?
